title: Alyeska sign, Kobuk Valley Jade.
description: Title taken from slide mount. View of a sign outside Alyeska Resort. The sign reads: "Kobuk Valley Jade Co. We take our name from the beautiful valley where the jade is found. In the center of Kobuk Valley, 150 miles north-east of Kotzebue, is the famous Jade Mountain. The jade is shipped down the Kobuk River by barge. The boulders you see were shipped over 2,000 miles. We make all our jewelry from this jade. From the Eskimos we get our ivory and other handcrafts. We have on display a unique collection of northwest Alaskan artifacts. Everyone is welcome. We hope our shop will add to your knowledge and enjoyment of Alaska."

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Jewel vein
description: Title taken from caption. "The Jewel Vein. This is what Frank Whitney found where he traced 'float' up the mountain. A true fissure vein with fine free walls. He has traced this vein 1500 ft. on the surface. We have driven 150 ft. of tunnel on it. The ore from full length of tunnel averages $71.00 a ton. This is the sort of vein that prospectors who know their business locate." (taken from back of print) A vein of ore going up a mountain.

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Sandbag levee along Seward Highway
description: Title and description taken from photograph caption. A sandbag levee constructed by soldiers of the 172nd Infantry Brigade, Fort Richardson, to prevent floating ice from entering the Seward Highway near Girdwood. Photo by PFC John Bacheller 172nd Infantry Brigade Fort Richardson, Alaska.

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Girdwood Hotel, 1956.
description: A view of the front of the Girdwood Hotel.  From verso: "Prop[r]ietors Ayers and Whitney. Beverly Staser in picture. Gene's Restaurant on left, Gene `The Frenchman' was long dead by 1956." The commercial signs on the front of the hotel read (top to bottom) Hamm's Beer and Schlitz.  Below the awning, next to the door is a sign reading Coca-Cola and a a sign in the window reads "Fine Food * Bar." Girdwood, Alaska, 1956.

title: Slide at mile 75 - Mr. Jones.
description:
Title from caption: "A.E.C. [Alaskan Engineering Commission], slide at m. 75 - Mr. Jones, 4/29/'20.

Location was determined based on the title given for UAF-1969-89-55. This avalanche is referenced in UAF-1969-89-56. Man wearing a fedora hat is shown walking on the tracks, holding his jacket and something that is shaped like a tube.  Snow is piled up on both sides of the tracks.

provenance.contributor: Alaska and Polar Regions Collections & Archives, Elmer E. Rasmuson Library, University of Alaska Fairbanks

title: Alyeska Resort Flowers.
description: Title taken from slide mount. View of flowers in pots and buckets on a wooden sidewalk in front of the Alyeska Resort. Hanging baskets of flowers are hanging from the deck. One woman is sitting at a picnic table underneath the deck eating, and two men are sitting in chairs near a payphone, also underneath the deck. A sign hanging from the deck reads: "Alyeska Sport Shop." There are also ladders, brooms, garbage cans, hoses, watering cans, and benches underneath the deck.

title: Gold panning at Girdwood.
description: Title taken from caption. Soldier in military issue hat panning for gold at Girdwood, Alaska. Also from caption: "Girdwood was an old gold mining area south of Anchorage several hours by rail. We took several excursions with USO. I mention it in some of my letters. We took many pictures and I have several kodachromes, too. The water color by Charlie Keck was of an old cabin at Girdwood." ca. 1945. Original photograph size: 4" x 5".

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Crow saddle.
description:
Title from photograph verso: Crow sadle.

Two saddled horses with one man on one of the horses.  The reference to "Crow" saddle could be referring to the saddle in the pass. There is a Cross Pass in Southcentral Alaska, but this area does not resemble it. There is also a Crow Pass in Southcentral Alaska, north of Girdwood, but it appears more mountainous than this photo.  Must be a different location as it looks like Interior Alaska.

provenance.contributor: Alaska and Polar Regions Collections & Archives, Elmer E. Rasmuson Library, University of Alaska Fairbanks

title: Chugach State Park: a proposal, circa 1969.
description: A pamphlet written by the Chugach Ad Hoc Committee, the Mountaineering Club of Alaska, and the Nordic Ski Club in support of the creation of a state park in the Anchorage area covering from Girdwood in the south to Eklutna in the north. The pamplet details the Committee's interest in creating the state park, the legislative means to establishing it, a map of the proposed boundaries, and some historical information about the location. To view the full text-searchable PDF, click on the download link or the expand button in the upper right of the image.

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508 mailto:archives@uaa.alaska.edu

title: Girdwood.
description: Title taken from caption with photograph. View of a waterfall and a river in Girdwood. The caption on the photograph album page with the image reads "Trip to Girdwood and gold mining camp. Smith - Hesseltine 'Sourdoughs'".

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Ground damage after 1964 earthquake.
description: View of damage after the March 27, 1964 earthquake.  Sign on top reads Girdwood. Vehicle seen in upper left. Location tentatively identified as in or near Girdwood, Alaska.

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Girdwood, 1956.
description: A view down a street in Girdwood facing west toward Turnagain arm and the Kenai Peninsula.  A banner hangs across the road.  The large white building to the right of the photograph is the Girdwood Hotel, with signs reading Hamm's Beer and Schlitz.  Girdwood, Alaska, 1956.

provenance.contributor: University of Alaska Anchorage. Consortium Library. Archives & Special Collections. 3211 Providence Dr. Anchorage, AK 99508, 1-907-786-1849. mailto:https://archives.consortiumlibrary.org/contact-us/

title: Panning Gold - Girdwood.
description: Title taken from caption with photograph. People are kneeling in front of a river or creek in Girdwood panning for gold.
